title: Qila Mubarak, Faridkot
tagline: Qila Mubarak, Faridkot — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Qila Mubarak, or Faridkot Fort, is a historic qila fortress located in Faridkot, Punjab, India that is spread over ten acres.
overview.description: Qila Mubarak, or Faridkot Fort, is a historic qila fortress located in Faridkot, Punjab, India that is spread over ten acres. It was associated with the princely-state of Faridkot. It is one of the few forts in familial possession that still survives in the region. The fortress was closed to public access for decades while its ownership was legally disputed.

topPlaces.name: Faridkot, India
topPlaces.description: Faridkot is a city in the south-western part of the state of Punjab, India. It serves as the headquarters for both the Faridkot district and the Faridkot Division.

topPlaces.name: Uranium poisoning in Punjab
topPlaces.description: Uranium poisoning in Punjab made news in 2009 when South African metal toxicologist Carin Smit ordered tests to be performed on children at Baba Farid Center For Special Children (BFCSC), a centre for children with autism, cerebral palsy, and other neurological disorders in Faridkot, Punjab. Lab tests found high levels of uranium in local children.

topPlaces.name: Faridkot (rural)
topPlaces.description: Faridkot (rural) is the largest village of Faridkot district located on the outskirts of the Faridkot City. According to the 2011 census, Faridkot had a population of 11,200.

items.note: Faridkot is a city in the south-western part of the state of Punjab, India. It serves as the headquarters for both the Faridkot district and the Faridkot Division.

Uranium poisoning in Punjab made news in 2009 when South African metal toxicologist Carin Smit ordered tests to be performed on children at Baba Farid Center For Special Children (BFCSC), a centre for children with autism, cerebral palsy, and other neurological disorders in Faridkot, Punjab. Lab tests found high levels of uranium in local children.
Faridkot (rural) is the largest village of Faridkot district located on the outskirts of the Faridkot City. According to the 2011 census, Faridkot had a population of 11,200.
Chahal is a village located in Faridkot district of Punjab, India.
Tehna (Punjabi: ਟਹਿਣਾ) is a village located in Faridkot district of Punjab, India.
Qila Nau (Punjabi: ਕਿਲਾ ਨੌ) is a village located in Faridkot district of Punjab State.
Bholuwala is a village in Faridkot district of Punjab, India.

Pakka (Punjabi: ਪੱਕਾ) is a village located in Faridkot district of Punjab State. The Village has a population of 4453 of which 2373 are males while 2080 are females as per the population census of 2011.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Qila Mubarak, Faridkot?
faq.a: The best time to visit Qila Mubarak, Faridkot is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Qila Mubarak, Faridkot?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar (115 km) and the nearest railway station is Amritsar Junction (107 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Qila Mubarak, Faridkot?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Qila Mubarak, Faridkot at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Qila Mubarak, Faridkot?
faq.a: Stays in Qila Mubarak, Faridkot start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Qila Mubarak, Faridkot?
faq.a: The top attractions are Faridkot, India, Uranium poisoning in Punjab, Faridkot (rural), Chahal (village), Tehna.
